questions.statement:
<p>Two identical circular wires P and Q each of radius R and carrying current I are kept in perpendicular planes such that they have a common centre as shown in figure. Find the magnitude and direction of the net magnetic field at the common centre of the two coils</p>

<p><img alt="" src="//cdn.testbook.com/images/production/quesImages/qImage67b832dea2bcd335657ebb55.png" /></p>

<!-- x-tinymce/html -->The coercivity of a bar magnet is 140 A m<sup>-1</sup>. To demagnetize it, it is placed inside a solenoid of length 1.6 m and number of turns 112. What is the current flowing through the solenoid?

Consider a long thin conducting wire carrying a uniform current I. A particle having mass &ldquo;M&rdquo; and charge &ldquo;q&rdquo; is released at a distance &ldquo;a&quot; from the wire with a speed v<sub>o</sub> along the direction of current in the wire. The particle gets attracted to the wire due to magnetic force. The particle turns round when it is at distance x from the wire. The value of x is [&mu;<sub>0</sub> is vacuum permeability]&nbsp;

A bar magnet having pole strength q<span style="position: relative;  line-height: 0; vertical-align: baseline; bottom: -0.25em;font-size:10.5px;">m</span>&nbsp;and magnetic moment m is divided into two equal parts along its length. The new pole strength is _________&nbsp;and the magnetic&nbsp;moment is&nbsp;_________&nbsp;respectively.

The magnetic field at a point P situated at perpendicular distance &#39;R&#39; from a long straight wire carrying a current of 12 A is 3 &times;&nbsp;10<span style="position: relative; line-height: 0; vertical-align: baseline; top: -0.5em; font-size:10.5px;">-5</span>&nbsp;Wb/m<span style="position: relative; line-height: 0; vertical-align: baseline; top: -0.5em; font-size:10.5px;">2</span>. The value of &#39;R&#39; in mm is [&mu;<span style="position: relative; line-height: 0; vertical-align: baseline; bottom: -0.25em; font-size:10.5px;">0&nbsp;</span>= 4&pi; &times; 10<span style="position: relative;  line-height: 0; vertical-align: baseline; top: -0.5em;font-size:10.5px;">-7</span>&nbsp;Wb/Am]

A jet plane of wing span 20 m is travelling towards west at a speed of 400 ms<sup>-1</sup>. If the earth&rsquo;s total magnetic field is 4 &times; 10<sup>-4</sup> T and the dip angle is 30&deg;, at that place, the voltage difference developed across the ends of the wing is

<p>A proton, a deuteron and an &alpha; - particle are projected perpendicular to the direction of a uniform magnetic field with same kinetic energy. The ratio of the radii of the circular paths described by them is</p>
At a point A on the earth&rsquo;s surface the angle of dip, &delta; = +25&deg;. At a point B on the earth&rsquo;s surface the angle of the dip, &delta; = -25&deg;. We can interpret that:
options: A and B are both located in the northern hemisphere. /
A is located in the southern hemisphere and B is located in the northern hemispher /
A is located in the northern hemisphere and B is located in the southern hemisphere /
A and B are both located in the southern hemisphere /

questions.statement: A current carrying wire of current 2A is placed in magnetic field of 24 Tesla. Find the force on the 3m length of the wire if the angle between wire and the field is 30&deg;.
What will be the magnetic dipole moment at the center, if a straight copper wire is bent into a circular loops of 50 turns with constant current 6 A and radius of 2 cm
